body.title: TWM sleep — background process evicts stale unintegrated observations
body.result: retired: superseded by T-twm-salience-time-decay + T-twm-leap-on-lever. No fold-in needed — decay demotes stale rows in eviction ranking; leap re-surfaces latent items when associatively relevant; narrative_engine already handles force-integration. The 'Igor feels sluggish' manual hotkey idea is debug-tooling, not ticket-worthy.
body.description:
Akien insight 2026-04-23: 'maybe we need a sleep thing that evicts stale memories.' The existing consolidation path (narrative_engine._integrate_gaps, _consolidation_merge_pass, _deep_consolidation_pass; cognition/consolidation.py run_consolidation) is demand-driven — runs when the narrative engine decides to, which depends on traffic and priorities. When Igor's been running a while and narrative engine hasn't kept up, TWM fills up with unintegrated high-salience observations and new pushes get silently dropped (see T-twm-self-eviction-policy).

**Framing:** biological analog — during wake, TWM accumulates; during sleep, TWM clears via consolidation + forgetting. Observations past some age that haven't been integrated either (a) get force-integrated in a batch, or (b) get aged out (salience decays past admit threshold). Either way, TWM makes room for the present.

**Proposed shape (for discussion, not yet decided):** a `twm_sleep()` pass that runs on Igor idle (separate from narrative engine's consolidation). Picks stale unintegrated rows (age > N minutes, not touched by any integration pass), force-integrates or drops them. Logs summary. Can be invoked manually too (for the "Igor feels sluggish, clear TWM" hotkey).

**Affected files:** TBD — discovery in sprint. Likely new wild_igor/igor/memory/twm_sleep.py + call site in main.py idle loop + manual invoker. Possibly cortex.py for a twm_stale_unintegrated() query helper.

**Design rules:** docs-in-code, test-plan-or-why-not, oop-first. Integrates with existing sleep_consolidation / sleep_clock subsystem — check if this is a new sibling or an extension of those.

**Scope boundary:** IN — one sleep-driven TWM eviction/integration pass with age + integration heuristics, manual invoker, log summary. OUT — changing the narrative engine's integration policy (this pass is BELOW that; a safety net, not a replacement), full biological-sleep state machine (that's a bigger architectural story).

**Test plan:** unit tests on the stale-picker heuristic (N-minute age + not-integrated returns expected rows, fresh rows spared); integration test: fill TWM with 50 unintegrated rows dated > N minutes ago, run twm_sleep, verify table returns to workable state and new pushes land. Observation: track SELF_EVICTED warning rate in prod logs before/after deploy.

**Sequence:** after T-twm-self-eviction-policy lands (so we have policy clarity on what "should not happen" vs what "needs sleep to prevent"). Not a technical gate — could also go in parallel.
